name: In-Hand Salary Calculator
description: Estimate your net salary received after standard deductions.
formula: In-hand = Basic + HRA + Other Allowances - Deductions
formulaDescription: Estimates net salary received in hand after subtracting deductions.

example.explanation: Basic + HRA + allowances minus deductions gives ₹75,000 in-hand.

faqs.question: Why is take-home different from salary?
faqs.answer: Salary - Income Tax - Professional Tax - Statutory Deductions = Take-home. Gross includes all components.

faqs.question: How is HRA exemption calculated?
faqs.answer: Lesser of: Actual HRA, 50% of salary (metro: 40%), or ₹15,000/month. Requires rent receipt.

faqs.question: What counts as income for tax?
faqs.answer: Salary (basic, DA, HRA, allowances), gifts >₹50K, prizes, capital gains, interest, rental income, business profit.

faqs.question: Is overtime taxable?
faqs.answer: Yes, overtime pay is regular income and fully taxable at your slab rate.

faqs.question: Can I negotiate salary in hand vs salary on paper?
faqs.answer: No, that's tax evasion. But you can optimize by adjusting component breakup and claiming valid deductions.

faqs.question: How much should I invest to reduce taxable income?
faqs.answer: Max benefit: ₹1.5L in 80C investments saves ~₹45K (30% slab). Invest in your goal timelines.

concept: Income calculators help you understand salary components, deductions, take-home pay, and various earning scenarios.
guideSteps: Enter your basic salary or hourly rate.
Add other income components (allowances, bonuses, stock options).
Input deductions if applicable (professional tax, health insurance).
Click Calculate for your take-home salary and tax breakdown.
Use to negotiate salaries, plan budgets, and understand pay slips.
standards: Basic salary: Core component; usually 40-50% of CTC.
HRA: Usually 10-15% of salary for metro cities.
Take-home: Gross salary - income tax - deductions.
